differentials.name: seizures, focal neurologic deficits Histo:  diffuse perivenous inflammatory process resulting in areas of demyelination Location:   subcortical white matter of both hemispheres asymmetrically; may involve brainstem / posterior fossa
differentials.raw: seizures, focal neurologic deficits Histo:  diffuse perivenous inflammatory process resulting in areas of demyelination Location:   subcortical white matter of both hemispheres asymmetrically; may involve brainstem / posterior fossa

differentials.name: pleocytosis
differentials.raw: pleocytosis (= ↑ WBC in spinal / other bodily fluid)

differentials.name: elevated protein in spinal fluid Location:   unilateral disease; parietal + posterior frontal white matter at level of centrum semiovale (sparing subcortical U-fibers + cortex) > basal ganglia, cerebellum, brainstem, spinal cord
differentials.raw: elevated protein in spinal fluid Location:   unilateral disease; parietal + posterior frontal white matter at level of centrum semiovale (sparing subcortical U-fibers + cortex) > basal ganglia, cerebellum, brainstem, spinal cord

differentials.raw: Hypertensive encephalopathy: eclampsia, thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura

context: =  POSTVIRAL LEUKOENCEPHALOPATHY =  autoimmune reaction against patient's white matter √  lesions may demonstrate contrast enhancement CT: √  multifocal hypodense white matter abnormalities √  sparing of cortical gray matter, occasionally deep gray matter involvement √  no additional lesions on follow-up exam MR: √  multifocal punctate / large confluent areas of hyperintensity on FLAIR / T2WI Rx:  corticosteroids result in dramatic improvement Prognosis:   complete resolution of neurologic deficits within 1 month (80-90%) / some permanent neurologic damage (10-20%) DDx:  multiple sclerosis (ra
